task_completion.description: Verify that the OpenBCI GUI application is open and running.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenBCI GUI main window is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Verify that a live Synthetic session is active.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Indicators of a live Synthetic session are visible, such as scrolling EEG waveforms, a 'Stop Data Stream' button, or session metadata indicating 'Synthetic' as the data source.

task_completion.description: Verify that the Hardware Settings panel is open.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A panel or modal labeled 'Hardware Settings' (or similar) is visible, displaying a grid or list of channels (1 through 8) and their configuration options.

task_completion.description: Verify that SRB2 is disabled for all odd channels (1, 3, 5, 7).
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Hardware Settings panel under the 'SRB2' column, the toggles, buttons, or indicators for channels 1, 3, 5, and 7 are set to the OFF or disabled state.

task_completion.description: Verify that SRB2 is enabled for all even channels (2, 4, 6, 8).
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Hardware Settings panel under the 'SRB2' column, the toggles, buttons, or indicators for channels 2, 4, 6, and 8 are set to the ON or enabled state.

task_completion.description: Verify that the screenshot of the configuration was saved to the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal, file manager, or image viewer shows the file 'srb2_config.png' exists in the directory '/home/ga/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Screenshots/', or the final provided image is the screenshot itself showing the correct configuration.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent interacted with the actual OpenBCI GUI application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show the genuine OpenBCI GUI interface with standard window decorations, rather than a text editor, mock-up, or terminal-only configuration.

integrity.description: Ensure the hardware settings UI matches the actual software version (v5.2.2) and is not fabricated.
integrity.visual_evidence: The column headers in the Hardware Settings panel should include 'PGA Gain', 'Input Type', 'Bias Include', 'SRB2', and 'SRB1'. There should NOT be a 'POWER' column.

integrity.description: Ensure the screenshot was actually taken from the live session.
integrity.visual_evidence: The saved screenshot should visually match the state of the GUI configured by the agent, showing the live synthetic data and the correct hardware settings panel open.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target SRB2 state requires odd channels (1, 3, 5, 7) to be OFF and even channels (2, 4, 6, 8) to be ON. The expected screenshot path is exactly '/home/ga/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Screenshots/srb2_config.png'. Note that in OpenBCI GUI v5.2.2, the hardware settings column headers are 'PGA Gain', 'Input Type', 'Bias Include', 'SRB2', and 'SRB1' (there is no 'POWER' column).
